growth_records.strain: Goodenough Lake (Cariboo Plateau, BC, Canada) environmental mat; carbon fixation attributed to MAG C5 Sodalinema (Nodosilinea C1 below detection)
growth_records.medium: Filtered Goodenough Lake water (alkaline soda lake) spiked with ~2% NaH13CO3

up.compound: bicarbonate/CO2 (inorganic carbon fixation, gross primary productivity)
up.method: 13C-bicarbonate stable isotope probing; IRMS on bulk dried biomass (gross daytime areal productivity)

growth_records.cite: Liu, Yihua et al. Exceptionally high carbon fixation and nitrogen assimilation rates in microbial mats of an alkaline soda lake. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf226. [PMC12598745]
growth_records.snippet: the approximate daytime gross productivity of the mat averaged 24 g C/m2/day, with a maximum of 33 g C/m2/day and a standard deviation of 5.4 ... From dried biomass, we calculated a gross bulk daytime productivity of 24 g C/m2/day (12 g C/m
growth_records.notes: Intact-mat daytime carbon fixation. Reported gross areal productivity = 24 g C/m2/day (avg), max 33, SD 5.4; equivalently 12 g C/m2/(12 h). Rate is areal (per m2 mat), NOT per gDW, so not convertible to mmol/gDW/h without unstated mat areal biomass density. Measured here (not cited) via 13C label increase in biomass (13C/12C rose from natural ~0.010924 to max 0.011294 at T7). Protein-SIP attributed the assimilation to Sodalinema (C5); Nodosilinea (C1) bicarbonate uptake below detection. Temperature not stated numerically (greenhouse, diffused sunlight); pH alkaline soda lake (not stated numerically in text).

growth_records.strain: Goodenough Lake (Cariboo Plateau, BC, Canada) environmental mat, homogenized slurry
growth_records.medium: Mimicked/native Goodenough Lake water with 0.5 mM 15N-labelled nitrogen supplement (non-N-limiting)

up.method: 15N-urea disappearance by GC-MS, linear regression of first 2 h (daytime)

up.compound: ammonium-N (assimilation, in presence of unlabelled nitrate)
nitrate-N (assimilation, in presence of unlabelled ammonium)

growth_records.cite: Liu, Yihua et al. Exceptionally high carbon fixation and nitrogen assimilation rates in microbial mats of an alkaline soda lake. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf226. [PMC12598745]
growth_records.snippet: The average daytime potential assimilation rate of urea-N was 5.5 umol/g/h, approximately three times higher than the ammonium assimilation rate, 1.8 umol/g/h and two times higher than the ammonium assimilation rate in the presence of nitra
growth_records.notes: Daytime homogenized-mat nitrogen assimilation, units umol N per gram dry biomass per hour. Distinct measured values: urea-N assimilation 5.5; urea-N consumption 6.5; ammonium-N (alone) 1.8; ammonium-N (with nitrate) 2.5; nitrate-N (with ammonium) 0.91. Cyanobacteria preferentially assimilate urea in the day. Nitrate assimilation in presence of ammonium was ~2x higher in day than night (night value not given numerically; would be ~0.46 if halved, NOT reported so left out). Temperature/pH not stated numerically.

growth_records.strain: Goodenough Lake (Cariboo Plateau, BC, Canada) environmental mat, homogenized slurry
growth_records.medium: Mimicked/native Goodenough Lake water with 0.5 mM 15N-labelled nitrogen supplement (non-N-limiting)

up.method: mat-slurry 15N2 assay (cited value, converted from 1674.3 nmol/g/day)

growth_records.cite: Liu, Yihua et al. Exceptionally high carbon fixation and nitrogen assimilation rates in microbial mats of an alkaline soda lake. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf226. [PMC12598745]
growth_records.snippet: The nighttime average ammonium assimilation rate, 2.5 umol/g/h, was slightly higher than during the daytime ... two and a half times higher than the nighttime urea-N assimilation rate, 0.98 umol/g/h ... urea consumption rate ... night, 5.5
growth_records.notes: Nighttime homogenized-mat nitrogen assimilation, umol N/g dry biomass/h. Measured here: ammonium-N 2.5 (29% higher than daytime 1.8, i.e. ammonium assimilation ~light-independent/higher at night); urea-N assimilation 0.98 (urea assimilation ~5.5x lower than day, consistent with photosynthesis-powered urea assimilation); urea-N consumption 5.5 (similar to daytime 6.5, so urea is consumed day AND night but assimilated mainly by day). N2-fixation-N ~0.14 umol/g/h is CITED from reference 41 (a mat-slurry 15N2 assay; original 1674.3 nmol/g/day) and NOT measured in this study -> is_experimental=false. Temperature/pH not stated numerically.
